limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified AI spend, headcount, or named AI infrastructure disclosed; score of 3 is based solely on the appointment of a Chief Digital Information Officer with an AI/ML background — this is an indirect signal of organizational commitment, not a disclosed budget or program.

ai_narrative_concreteness: AI is discussed only in competitive-risk and cybersecurity-risk contexts; no named internal AI initiatives, deployment status, or use-case metrics are disclosed. Language is largely aspirational/defensive.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Two named risk categories are identified (competitive disadvantage from AI adoption lag; data/confidentiality risk from AI use), but no quantified risk scenarios or named governance bodies are disclosed, capping the score at 3.

Excerpts are limited to risk_factors and item_4 sections; no MD&A, business description, or capex discussion sections are present, which may understate investment intensity and narrative concreteness.
